title: Dynamically disabling SSR for the provider can suppress SSR output for all wrapped children

evidence.quote:
This prevents wagmi / WalletConnect / idb-keyval from being evaluated
 * during SSR/SSG

reasoning: The component disables SSR for the entire dynamically imported Providers component and passes the page subtree as that dynamic component's children. In Next, an ssr:false dynamic component is not rendered during SSR/SSG, so if ProvidersClient is used as an app/root wrapper, the wrapped children can be omitted from the server-rendered HTML and only appear after client hydration. That is a correctness and release risk for first paint, SEO, static output, and no-JS behavior. The stated goal is only to avoid evaluating wallet/idb code during SSR, but this implementation can move the entire child UI behind a client-only boundary.
recommendation: Keep the children SSR-renderable and isolate only browser-only wallet/provider initialization behind a client-only boundary. For example, make Providers itself safe to import on the server by moving idb/WalletConnect access into effects or nested client-only components, or render a server-safe passthrough shell that returns children during SSR and mounts the wallet providers on the client without hiding the subtree.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for SSR/SSG output or hydration behavior of ProvidersClient.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Next render/build test for a page wrapped by ProvidersClient that asserts representative child content exists in the server-rendered HTML/static output before client hydration.
minimumFixScope: frontend/src/components/providers-client.tsx and, if needed, the imported providers implementation to split server-safe rendering from browser-only wallet initialization.
